sectionContents.aufnahmeanlass: Emergency psychiatric admission via liaison psychiatry after an urgent GP referral. Acute psychotic decompensation with persecutory beliefs, alcohol withdrawal features, and progressive sleep deprivation. No immediate risk to others on presentation; limited illness insight and moderate alcohol withdrawal risk.
sectionContents.aktuelle-beschwerden: For roughly two weeks: escalating suspicion that neighbours and former flatmates are monitoring him (CCTV, “listening devices”), sleeping under 3 hours per night, repeated contact with local police without substantiated incidents. Concurrent daily alcohol use (~8–12 UK units beer/day, often morning “steady” drinks); last drink 16 h before admission. Tremor, sweating, subjective restlessness. Mood fluctuating between anxious and irritable. Appetite poor.
sectionContents.eigenanamnese: Mr Demo provides a reliable self-history once rapport is established. No relevant somatic comorbidity known. No known drug allergies.
sectionContents.aktuelle-krankheitsanamnese: First psychotic episode at age 28 during a period of heavy drinking and warehouse overtime; brief admission, then several outpatient episodes with patchy antipsychotic adherence. Current relapse in the context of sleep loss, alcohol withdrawal, and job loss (made redundant from logistics role four months ago).
sectionContents.psychiatrische-vorgeschichte: Multiple prior contacts; last community mental health review six months ago for alcohol detox planning. Previous working diagnoses: paranoid schizophrenia (F20.0), alcohol dependence (F10.2). No suicide attempts. No prolonged involuntary detention.
sectionContents.somatische-anamnese: Epigastric discomfort with drinking. Liver enzymes intermittently borderline (GGT). No known cirrhosis. No known medication allergies.
sectionContents.suchtanamnese: Alcohol since late teens; currently ~8–12 units beer daily with morning use to “settle nerves”. Several unsuccessful community detox attempts. Withdrawal when abstinent: tremor, sweating, agitation, insomnia. Nicotine ~20 cigarettes/day. No regular illicit drug use in the past year.
sectionContents.medikamentenanamnese: Before admission: intermittent risperidone 2 mg nocte (adherence uncertain), occasional lorazepam for agitation. No regular intake in the fortnight before admission. No community detox prescription.
sectionContents.familienanamnese: Father with treated alcohol problems (details unclear). No known family psychosis. Mother well. Older sister supportive but lives in another city.
sectionContents.biografische-anamnese: Urban upbringing; GCSE-level education; warehouse and logistics roles since early twenties. Long-term partner relationship ended eight months ago.
sectionContents.sozialanamnese: Lives alone in a council flat (synthetic demo address, fictional). On Universal Credit since redundancy; rent arrears for two months. Social contact reduced; increasing isolation.
sectionContents.schul-und-berufsanamnese: Warehouse picker / logistics until redundancy for lateness and drinking on shift. Currently unemployed; Jobcentre appointment scheduled.
sectionContents.forensische-anamnese: No relevant forensic history. No pending criminal proceedings. Police contacts related to noise complaints and neighbour disputes.
sectionContents.traumaanamnese: No clear trauma reported. Adolescent stressors poorly defined; possible family conflict in context of paternal alcohol use.
sectionContents.suizid-und-selbstgefaehrdungsanamnese: Passive death wishes occasionally ("sometimes peace would be good") but no concrete plans, intent, or preparation. No prior suicide attempt. Protective factors: ambivalent tie to brother; therapeutic alliance slowly forming. BRMS screen: low acute risk; safety plan discussed.
sectionContents.fremdgefaehrdungsanamnese: No indicators of acute risk to others. No weapons. Occasional loud behaviour; neighbour complaints.
sectionContents.psychopathologischer-befund: Awake, oriented ×4. Contact effortful, suspicious. Affect labile, partly irritable. Thought form mildly circumstantial; content delusional-paranoid (persecutory and surveillance ideas without full insight). No clear hallucinations at examination. Drive reduced. Sleep disturbed. Illness insight low. Mild hand tremor.
sectionContents.somatischer-befund: Vitals: BP 142/88 mmHg, pulse 96/min, temp. 36.8 °C, SpO₂ 98%. Heart and lungs unremarkable. Abdomen soft, mild epigastric tenderness. Mild tremor. Brief neurological exam without focal deficit.
sectionContents.neurologischer-befund: Alert and oriented ×4. Pupils equal and reactive. No focal neurological deficits. Gait slightly unsteady with tremor, no ataxia. No meningeal signs. Reflexes symmetric.
sectionContents.diagnostische-einschaetzung: Working diagnosis: acute psychotic episode in paranoid schizophrenia (F20.0) with comorbid alcohol dependence (F10.2); differential: alcohol-induced psychosis and schizoaffective disorder. Diagnostic uncertainty — further observation and abstinence required.
sectionContents.therapieplanung-behandlungsplan: Antipsychotic stabilisation, structured alcohol withdrawal management (CIWA-Ar monitoring), benzodiazepine protocol PRN. Substance-motivation work and discharge planning with social services. Psychoeducation and progress documentation.

sectionContents.psychopathologie: Fluctuating mood, paranoid-mistrustful baseline, recently calmer. Withdrawal symptoms declining.
sectionContents.stationsverhalten: Partially engaged in conversations, attended exercise therapy.
sectionContents.risiko: No acute suicidality or risk to others. Withdrawal risk monitored.
sectionContents.compliance-krankheitseinsicht: Currently regular intake, insight slowly increasing. Alcohol abstinence since admission.
sectionContents.somatik: GGT elevated at admission, declining on follow-up. Prolactin normalised after switch.
sectionContents.beurteilung-plan: Plan outpatient continuation; link addiction counselling.

sectionContents.free: Awake and fully oriented. Affect labile. Thought content paranoid. No hallucinatory experiences at time of examination. Mild tremor.

sectionContents.body: Stabilisation on antipsychotic, structured alcohol withdrawal management, psychoeducation group, exercise therapy.
See medication plan — aripiprazole 10 mg, lorazepam PRN.
Stabilisation, alcohol use work, discharge preparation with social services.

diagnosticMappings.label: Paranoid schizophrenia
criteriaMet: Anhaltender, kulturell unangemessener und völlig unrealistischer (bizarrer) Wahn
Gedankenabreißen oder Einschiebungen in den Gedankenfluss mit Zerfahrenheit, Danebenreden oder Neologismen
Die charakteristischen Symptome bestehen die meiste Zeit während eines Zeitraums von mindestens einem Monat
criteriaMissing: Ich-Störungen: Gedankenlautwerden, Gedankeneingebung, -entzug oder -ausbreitung sowie Kontroll- bzw. Beeinflussungserleben oder Gefühl des Gemachten (Passivitätserleben)
Kommentierende oder dialogische (über die Person sprechende) Stimmen bzw. andere anhaltende Stimmen aus einem Körperteil
exclusions: Bei gleichzeitig ausgeprägter manischer oder depressiver Symptomatik gingen die schizophrenen Symptome der affektiven Störung voraus (kein vorrangiges affektives Zustandsbild)

criteriaMissing: Starkes Verlangen oder eine Art Zwang, Alkohol zu konsumieren (Craving)
Verminderte Kontrolle über Beginn, Beendigung und Menge des Konsums
Toleranzentwicklung mit erforderlicher Dosissteigerung für die ursprüngliche Wirkung
Zunehmende Vernachlässigung anderer Interessen und Aktivitäten zugunsten des Konsums sowie erhöhter Zeitaufwand für Beschaffung, Konsum und Erholung
Anhaltender Konsum trotz Nachweises eindeutig schädlicher körperlicher, psychischer oder sozialer Folgen
differentials: Schädlicher Gebrauch / riskanter Konsum (F10.1) ohne Abhängigkeit

verlaufFeed.content: Admission to ward. Patient suspicious, minimal sleep, mild tremor. Risperidone 2 mg fixed; lorazepam PRN prescribed. CIWA-Ar baseline 14 — structured withdrawal monitoring started.
Afternoon increasing agitation and tremor; CIWA-Ar 18. Lorazepam 1 mg once. Contact remains tense; paranoid themes present.
Night sleep 4 h on lorazepam. Paranoid themes still present but less agitated. CIWA-Ar 11 — withdrawal symptoms declining.
Psychoeducation group attended; passive participation. Alcohol withdrawal and medication discussed.
Risperidone increased to 3 mg for persistent positive symptoms. CIWA-Ar 8.
Admission labs (05 Jun) — prolactin markedly elevated on risperidone 3 mg; GGT borderline elevated (alcohol history); routine panel otherwise unremarkable.
Individual session: patient reports reduced "surveillance" experiences; sleep 5–6 h. Motivational interview on alcohol abstinence — ambivalent but open.
Ward behaviour calmer. Withdrawal symptoms largely resolved. Motivation for discharge planning still low.
ECG unremarkable (sinus rhythm, QTc 412 ms). Meeting with social services re housing, rent arrears, and employment.
Switch to aripiprazole 10 mg started (risperidone tapered) due to prolactin rise and amotivation. Alcohol abstinence since admission (day 7).
Exercise therapy attended twice. Mood somewhat improved. Smoking reduction discussed (nicotine 20/day).
No acute suicidality. Illness insight slowly increasing. Safety plan updated. Withdrawal prophylaxis: patient declined disulfiram, open to naltrexone discussion outpatient.
Resting EEG (11 Jun): mild diffuse slowing; no epileptiform discharges.
AIMS check: mild akathisia, no dyskinesia. SAS 1/1/0. GGT declining on follow-up.
Follow-up labs (20 Jun) — prolactin normalised after switch to aripiprazole; aripiprazole level in therapeutic range. Triglycerides and HbA1c borderline — continue metabolic monitoring.
Discharge planning: outpatient follow-up (psychiatry + addiction counselling), housing security pending, medication stabilised on aripiprazole 10 mg + lorazepam PRN. Alcohol abstinence 12 days.

verlaufAnnotations.comment: Consultant: continue structured withdrawal monitoring for first 72 h; nursing to document CIWA-Ar q4h.

verlaufAnnotations.todoText: Repeat CIWA-Ar at 18:00 and notify medical if score ≥15

verlaufAnnotations.comment: Discussed prolactin and EPS risk with patient — partial insight, ambivalent about long-term antipsychotic.

verlaufAnnotations.todoText: Arrange addiction counselling referral before discharge

verlaufAnnotations.comment: OT note: good engagement; consider maintaining referral outpatient.
Sister listed as protective contact; housing letter from social services pending.

verlaufAnnotations.todoText: Confirm landlord payment plan with social services

dokumente.title: History — admission
dokumente.content: Complete admission history (demo) — paranoid schizophrenia with alcohol dependence.

sectionContents.aufnahmeanlass: Emergency psychiatric admission via liaison psychiatry after an urgent GP referral. Acute psychotic decompensation with persecutory beliefs, alcohol withdrawal features, and progressive sleep deprivation. No immediate risk to others on presentation; limited illness insight and moderate alcohol withdrawal risk.
sectionContents.aktuelle-beschwerden: For roughly two weeks: escalating suspicion that neighbours and former flatmates are monitoring him (CCTV, “listening devices”), sleeping under 3 hours per night, repeated contact with local police without substantiated incidents. Concurrent daily alcohol use (~8–12 UK units beer/day, often morning “steady” drinks); last drink 16 h before admission. Tremor, sweating, subjective restlessness. Mood fluctuating between anxious and irritable. Appetite poor.
sectionContents.eigenanamnese: Mr Demo provides a reliable self-history once rapport is established. No relevant somatic comorbidity known. No known drug allergies.
sectionContents.aktuelle-krankheitsanamnese: First psychotic episode at age 28 during a period of heavy drinking and warehouse overtime; brief admission, then several outpatient episodes with patchy antipsychotic adherence. Current relapse in the context of sleep loss, alcohol withdrawal, and job loss (made redundant from logistics role four months ago).
sectionContents.psychiatrische-vorgeschichte: Multiple prior contacts; last community mental health review six months ago for alcohol detox planning. Previous working diagnoses: paranoid schizophrenia (F20.0), alcohol dependence (F10.2). No suicide attempts. No prolonged involuntary detention.
sectionContents.somatische-anamnese: Epigastric discomfort with drinking. Liver enzymes intermittently borderline (GGT). No known cirrhosis. No known medication allergies.
sectionContents.suchtanamnese: Alcohol since late teens; currently ~8–12 units beer daily with morning use to “settle nerves”. Several unsuccessful community detox attempts. Withdrawal when abstinent: tremor, sweating, agitation, insomnia. Nicotine ~20 cigarettes/day. No regular illicit drug use in the past year.
sectionContents.medikamentenanamnese: Before admission: intermittent risperidone 2 mg nocte (adherence uncertain), occasional lorazepam for agitation. No regular intake in the fortnight before admission. No community detox prescription.
sectionContents.familienanamnese: Father with treated alcohol problems (details unclear). No known family psychosis. Mother well. Older sister supportive but lives in another city.
sectionContents.biografische-anamnese: Urban upbringing; GCSE-level education; warehouse and logistics roles since early twenties. Long-term partner relationship ended eight months ago.
sectionContents.sozialanamnese: Lives alone in a council flat (synthetic demo address, fictional). On Universal Credit since redundancy; rent arrears for two months. Social contact reduced; increasing isolation.
sectionContents.schul-und-berufsanamnese: Warehouse picker / logistics until redundancy for lateness and drinking on shift. Currently unemployed; Jobcentre appointment scheduled.
sectionContents.forensische-anamnese: No relevant forensic history. No pending criminal proceedings. Police contacts related to noise complaints and neighbour disputes.
sectionContents.traumaanamnese: No clear trauma reported. Adolescent stressors poorly defined; possible family conflict in context of paternal alcohol use.
sectionContents.suizid-und-selbstgefaehrdungsanamnese: Passive death wishes occasionally ("sometimes peace would be good") but no concrete plans, intent, or preparation. No prior suicide attempt. Protective factors: ambivalent tie to brother; therapeutic alliance slowly forming. BRMS screen: low acute risk; safety plan discussed.
sectionContents.fremdgefaehrdungsanamnese: No indicators of acute risk to others. No weapons. Occasional loud behaviour; neighbour complaints.
sectionContents.psychopathologischer-befund: Awake, oriented ×4. Contact effortful, suspicious. Affect labile, partly irritable. Thought form mildly circumstantial; content delusional-paranoid (persecutory and surveillance ideas without full insight). No clear hallucinations at examination. Drive reduced. Sleep disturbed. Illness insight low. Mild hand tremor.
sectionContents.somatischer-befund: Vitals: BP 142/88 mmHg, pulse 96/min, temp. 36.8 °C, SpO₂ 98%. Heart and lungs unremarkable. Abdomen soft, mild epigastric tenderness. Mild tremor. Brief neurological exam without focal deficit.
sectionContents.neurologischer-befund: Alert and oriented ×4. Pupils equal and reactive. No focal neurological deficits. Gait slightly unsteady with tremor, no ataxia. No meningeal signs. Reflexes symmetric.
sectionContents.diagnostische-einschaetzung: Working diagnosis: acute psychotic episode in paranoid schizophrenia (F20.0) with comorbid alcohol dependence (F10.2); differential: alcohol-induced psychosis and schizoaffective disorder. Diagnostic uncertainty — further observation and abstinence required.
sectionContents.therapieplanung-behandlungsplan: Antipsychotic stabilisation, structured alcohol withdrawal management (CIWA-Ar monitoring), benzodiazepine protocol PRN. Substance-motivation work and discharge planning with social services. Psychoeducation and progress documentation.

dokumente.title: Progress summary
dokumente.content:
Admission to ward. Patient suspicious, minimal sleep, mild tremor. Risperidone 2 mg fixed; lorazepam PRN prescribed. CIWA-Ar baseline 14 — structured withdrawal monitoring started.

Afternoon increasing agitation and tremor; CIWA-Ar 18. Lorazepam 1 mg once. Contact remains tense; paranoid themes present.

Night sleep 4 h on lorazepam. Paranoid themes still present but less agitated. CIWA-Ar 11 — withdrawal symptoms declining.

Psychoeducation group attended; passive participation. Alcohol withdrawal and medication discussed.

Somatic examination — admission (day 2)

Risperidone increased to 3 mg for persistent positive symptoms. CIWA-Ar 8.

Admission labs (05 Jun) — prolactin markedly elevated on risperidone 3 mg; GGT borderline elevated (alcohol history); routine panel otherwise unremarkable.

Individual session: patient reports reduced "surveillance" experiences; sleep 5–6 h. Motivational interview on alcohol abstinence — ambivalent but open.

Ward behaviour calmer. Withdrawal symptoms largely resolved. Motivation for discharge planning still low.

ECG unremarkable (sinus rhythm, QTc 412 ms). Meeting with social services re housing, rent arrears, and employment.

Switch to aripiprazole 10 mg started (risperidone tapered) due to prolactin rise and amotivation. Alcohol abstinence since admission (day 7).

Exercise therapy attended twice. Mood somewhat improved. Smoking reduction discussed (nicotine 20/day).

No acute suicidality. Illness insight slowly increasing. Safety plan updated. Withdrawal prophylaxis: patient declined disulfiram, open to naltrexone discussion outpatient.

Resting EEG (11 Jun): mild diffuse slowing; no epileptiform discharges.

AIMS check: mild akathisia, no dyskinesia. SAS 1/1/0. GGT declining on follow-up.

Follow-up labs (20 Jun) — prolactin normalised after switch to aripiprazole; aripiprazole level in therapeutic range. Triglycerides and HbA1c borderline — continue metabolic monitoring.

Somatic examination — follow-up (day 11)

Discharge planning: outpatient follow-up (psychiatry + addiction counselling), housing security pending, medication stabilised on aripiprazole 10 mg + lorazepam PRN. Alcohol abstinence 12 days.

findings.mechanism: Both agents have central depressant effects; benzodiazepines amplify sedating antipsychotic effects.
findings.monitoring: Sedation, dizziness, fall risk; monitor respiration with high benzodiazepine doses
findings.clinicalManagement: Use lorazepam PRN at lowest effective dose; counsel on impaired driving.

findings.mechanism: Partial D2 agonism plus GABA-A modulation — clinically relevant with concurrent use.
findings.monitoring: Daytime functioning, fall log, document PRN frequency
findings.clinicalManagement: Maintain PRN schedule; consider alternative anxiolysis if repeated need.

findings.mechanism: Risperidone blocks tuberoinfundibular D2 receptors → prolactin rise
findings.recommendation: Switch to aripiprazole already completed; repeat prolactin in 2–4 weeks.
findings.monitoring: Prolactin, clinical symptoms if applicable (galactorrhoea, libido loss)

activeDimensions.longitudinalPattern: Escalated over 2 weeks with sleep loss and daily alcohol use; partial response after antipsychotic switch.
activeDimensions.clinicalSummary: Persistent persecutory and surveillance beliefs with limited insight; content aligns with first-rank symptom cluster under stress.
activeDimensions.uncertainty: Substance contribution vs primary psychosis remains partially unresolved.
activeDimensions.missingData: Collateral history from outpatient team not yet available.

activeDimensions.longitudinalPattern: Severe sleep restriction (<3 h/night) preceded decompensation; improving to 5–6 h on ward.
activeDimensions.clinicalSummary: Marked insomnia and circadian disruption — likely both precipitant and maintaining factor.

activeDimensions.longitudinalPattern: Daily alcohol (~0.5–1 L wine) until admission; abstinence since day of admission.
activeDimensions.clinicalSummary: Active substance use likely lowered threshold for psychotic relapse; abstinence is a treatment target.
activeDimensions.uncertainty: Timeline does not fully explain 5-week symptom course alone.
activeDimensions.missingData: Quantitative toxicology panel pending at discharge.

activeDimensions.longitudinalPattern: Situational anxiety in public spaces; overlaps with paranoid ideation.
activeDimensions.clinicalSummary: Heightened threat anticipation — may amplify misinterpretation of neutral social cues.

activeDimensions.longitudinalPattern: Reduced drive and social withdrawal over past 3 months.
activeDimensions.clinicalSummary: Avolition and social disengagement present but less prominent than positive symptoms.
activeDimensions.uncertainty: May improve as sleep and substance use stabilise.

activeDimensions.longitudinalPattern: Labile affect between irritable and anxious poles during admission.
activeDimensions.clinicalSummary: Emotional lability without sustained manic episode — monitor for mood polarity.

activeDimensions.longitudinalPattern: Job loss and housing stress 3 months before admission; occupational function impaired.
activeDimensions.clinicalSummary: Psychosocial stressors and functional decline — discharge planning must address housing and work.

exploratoryInsufficientEvidence.rationale: No clear trauma narrative documented — insufficient evidence for dimensional scoring.

activeMechanisms.label: Dopamine / Aberrant Salience Dysregulation
activeMechanisms.clinicalImplication: Positive symptoms and salience disturbances fit mesolimbic dopamine dysregulation — antipsychotic response supports this pathway.
activeMechanisms.treatmentRelevance: Continue partial D2 agonist (aripiprazole); monitor for akathisia. Avoid prolactin-elevating agents given prior hyperprolactinaemia on risperidone.
activeMechanisms.uncertainty: Stimulant-induced dopamine surge may have contributed acutely.

activeMechanisms.label: Stress-System / HPA Axis Dysregulation
activeMechanisms.clinicalImplication: Chronic psychosocial stress and sleep deprivation may have sensitized stress-response systems.
activeMechanisms.treatmentRelevance: Sleep hygiene, stress management, and structured daily routine as adjuncts to antipsychotic therapy.
activeMechanisms.uncertainty: No cortisol sampling in this demo dataset.

activeMechanisms.label: Glutamate–GABA Dysconnectivity
activeMechanisms.clinicalImplication: Mild formal thought disorder suggests E/I imbalance may contribute alongside dopamine pathways.
activeMechanisms.treatmentRelevance: Consider NMDA-modulating strategies only within specialist pathways — not first-line here.

activeMechanisms.label: Circadian / Sleep–Wake Dysregulation
activeMechanisms.clinicalImplication: Sleep loss is a plausible precipitant of psychotic decompensation in this case.
activeMechanisms.treatmentRelevance: Prioritise sleep stabilisation; limit PRN benzodiazepine to avoid dependence.

audit.notes: Pre-baked demo Clinical Intelligence run (synthetic, no live API).

clinicalIntelligence.clinicianComment: Demo review: dimensional profile supports psychotic decompensation with sleep and substance contributors. Accept mechanisms after ward round.
